 {"id":"rich_rust-01n","title":"Validate Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13","description":"## Objective
Verify the text wrapping implementation matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 13 exactly.

## Background
Text wrapping is critical for responsive terminal output. The algorithm must handle word boundaries, long words, wide characters, and styled text spans correctly.

## Validation Checklist

### 13.1 Word Boundary Detection
- [ ] Verify words split on whitespace
- [ ] Verify hyphenated words treated as single unit (or split at hyphen)
- [ ] Verify CJK characters can wrap between any characters

### 13.2 Wrap Algorithm
- [ ] Verify words fit: add to current line
- [ ] Verify word too long: break at width (fold mode)
- [ ] Verify line break: start new line, preserve indent
- [ ] Verify whitespace at line break: consumed (not carried)

### 13.3 Overflow Methods
- [ ] Verify Fold: break anywhere when word > width
- [ ] Verify Crop: truncate at width
- [ ] Verify Ellipsis: truncate at width-1 and add ellipsis

### 13.4 Style Preservation
- [ ] Verify spans are split correctly across line breaks
- [ ] Verify style continues on wrapped lines
- [ ] Verify partial spans at wrap point

### 13.5 Edge Cases
- [ ] Test wrapping with wide characters (2-cell)
- [ ] Test wrapping with zero-width characters
- [ ] Test wrapping at exactly width boundary
- [ ] Test no_wrap flag
- [ ] Test empty text
- [ ] Test single long word

## Files to Examine
- src/text.rs (wrap methods)
- src/cells.rs (width calculation)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 13","acceptance_criteria":"All wrap scenarios work correctly. Styles preserved across line breaks.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:12.64426047-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:44:58.001495493-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:44:58.001495493-05:00","close_reason":"Validated Text Wrapping against RICH_SPEC Section 13. All 41 E2E tests and 17 unit tests pass. Implementation correctly handles: word boundary detection via character iteration, wrap algorithm with proper line division, all 4 overflow methods (Fold, Crop, Ellipsis, Ignore), style preservation through slice()/divide(), and edge cases (empty text, whitespace, zero width, wide chars). No gaps found.","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-01n","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:38.498007011-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
-{"id":"rich_rust-02i","title":"Verify Markup Parser Error Recovery","description":"## Objective
Ensure markup parser gracefully handles malformed input as specified in RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.

## Background
Users will make mistakes in markup. The parser should render as much as possible and treat invalid markup as literal text rather than crashing.

## Technical Details

### Error Recovery Cases

#### Unclosed Tags
Input: '[bold]hello'
Expected: render 'hello' with bold, auto-close at end

#### Unmatched Close Tags
Input: 'hello[/bold]'
Expected: render '[/bold]' as literal text

#### Invalid Style in Tag
Input: '[invalidstyle]hello[/]'
Expected: render '[invalidstyle]hello[/]' as literal OR apply null style

#### Nested Mismatches
Input: '[bold][italic]hello[/bold][/italic]'
Expected: reasonable behavior (spec should define)

#### Empty Tags
Input: '[]hello'
Expected: render '[]hello' as literal OR ignore empty tag

#### Escaped Brackets
Input: 'price is \[100\]'
Expected: render 'price is [100]'

### Verification Steps
1. Write test for each error case
2. Compare to Python Rich behavior
3. Document any intentional deviations
4. Ensure no panics/crashes on any input

## Files to Examine
- src/markup/mod.rs (parser)
- Python Rich markup.py (reference)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 4.4","acceptance_criteria":"Parser handles all malformed input gracefully. No panics.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:31.584465299-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:22:31.584465299-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.94065411-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-02i","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-qgs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:43:22.987009673-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-0sw","title":"Validate Table Rendering against RICH_SPEC Section 9.5-9.6","description":"## Objective
Verify Table rendering (row rendering, border drawing) matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.5-9.6.

## Background
This covers the visual output of tables: how rows are rendered, how borders are drawn, header/footer handling.

## Validation Checklist

### 9.5 Row Rendering
- [ ] render_row() produces correct segments
- [ ] Cell content is wrapped to column width
- [ ] Vertical alignment (top/middle/bottom) applied correctly
- [ ] Padding applied inside cells
- [ ] Multi-line cells: all lines padded to same width
- [ ] Border characters inserted between columns
- [ ] Row style applied to cell backgrounds

### 9.6 Full Table Rendering
- [ ] Title rendered above table (centered by default)
- [ ] Header row uses header_style
- [ ] Header row separator uses head_row from BoxChars
- [ ] Body rows alternate row_styles if provided
- [ ] Row separators (when show_lines=true) use row from BoxChars
- [ ] Footer row uses footer from BoxChars
- [ ] Caption rendered below table
- [ ] Edge borders controlled by show_edge

### Box Character Usage
- [ ] Top border: box.top (only if show_edge)
- [ ] Header separator: box.head_row
- [ ] Row separator: box.row (only if show_lines)
- [ ] Footer separator: box.foot_row (if footer present)
- [ ] Bottom border: box.bottom (only if show_edge)
- [ ] Column dividers: box.vertical

### Edge Cases
- [ ] Empty table (no rows)
- [ ] No header (show_header=false)
- [ ] No edge borders (show_edge=false)
- [ ] No row lines (show_lines=false)
- [ ] Single column table
- [ ] Single row table

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (rendering functions)
- src/box.rs (BoxChars)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.5-9.6","acceptance_criteria":"Table renders correctly with all combinations of options.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:42.754066074-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T13:21:01.430656253-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T13:21:01.430656253-05:00","close_reason":"## Validation Complete - Table Rendering

### Summary
Table rendering implementation is **MOSTLY ALIGNED** with RICH_SPEC.md Sections 9.5-9.6. The main limitation is **single-line cell rendering only** - multi-line cells with vertical alignment are not supported.

---

### 9.5 Full Table Rendering ✅ ALIGNED

**render() function (lines 641-738)** follows spec structure:

| Step | Spec | Impl | Status |
|------|------|------|--------|
| Calculate widths | calculate_column_widths() | calculate_widths() | ✅ |
| Render title | if self.title | if let Some(title) | ✅ |
| Top border | if show_edge | if self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Header | if show_header | if self.show_header | ✅ |
| Header separator | head_sep | HeadRow level | ✅ |
| Data rows | iterate rows | iterate self.rows | ✅ |
| Row styles | alternating row_styles | row_styles mod | ✅ |
| Row separators | if show_lines/end_section | if show_lines/end_section | ✅ |
| Footer | if show_footer | if self.show_footer | ✅ |
| Footer separator | foot_sep | FootRow level | ✅ |
| Bottom border | if show_edge | if self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Caption | if self.caption | if let Some(caption) | ✅ |

---

### 9.6 Row Rendering ⚠️ SIMPLIFIED

**render_row_content() (lines 797-890)** is simplified vs spec's render_row():

| Feature | Spec | Impl | Status |
|---------|------|------|--------|
| Single-line cells | ✅ | ✅ | ✅ |
| Multi-line cells | Lines array | Not supported | ⚠️ MISSING |
| Vertical alignment | align_top/middle/bottom | Not implemented | ⚠️ MISSING |
| Content wrapping | column width | Truncates instead | ⚠️ DIFFERS |
| Horizontal padding | pad_str | pad_str | ✅ |
| Cell dividers | box_style.head[2] | box_chars.head[2] | ✅ |
| Left/right edges | show_edge | self.show_edge | ✅ |
| Style combination | row_style + cell_style | combined_style | ✅ |

**Impact**: Tables with long content are truncated, not wrapped. Cells cannot span multiple lines.

---

### Box Character Usage ✅ CORRECT

| Border Type | Spec RowLevel | Impl RowLevel | Status |
|-------------|---------------|---------------|--------|
| Top border | Top | Top | ✅ |
| Header sep | Head | HeadRow | ✅ |
| Row sep | Row | Row | ✅ |
| Footer sep | Foot | FootRow | ✅ |
| Bottom border | Bottom | Bottom | ✅ |
| Cell dividers | head[2] | head[2] | ✅ |

BoxChars struct (src/box.rs) has all required character arrays:
- ASCII, SQUARE, ROUNDED, DOUBLE box styles ✅
- get_row_chars() method ✅
- build_row() method ✅

---

### Edge Cases ✅ TESTED

E2E tests cover:
- Empty table (e2e_table_empty) ✅
- Single column (e2e_table_single_column) ✅
- Single cell (e2e_table_single_cell) ✅
- Empty cells (e2e_table_empty_cells) ✅
- No header (show_header=false) ✅
- No edges (show_edge=false) ✅
- Row lines (show_lines=true) ✅
- Footer (show_footer=true) ✅

---

### Overall Assessment: ✅ PASS (with simplifications)

**Correct**:
- Full table rendering structure matches spec
- Box character usage correct
- Edge cases handled
- All configuration options work

**Simplified/Missing**:
1. **Multi-line cells**: Not supported (content truncated)
2. **Vertical alignment**: Column.vertical field exists but not used
3. **Text wrapping**: Content exceeding width is truncated, not wrapped

These simplifications are acceptable for Phase 1 but should be tracked for future enhancement.","labels":["phase-1","validation"]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-0y1","title":"Add Thread Safety Verification","description":"## Objective
Verify and document thread safety of all public types.

## Background
Rust libraries should be clear about thread safety. Any global state (like caches) must be properly synchronized.

## Audit Areas

### Global State
- [ ] Color cache (if using global LRU cache) - needs Mutex
- [ ] Cell width cache (if using global) - needs Mutex or thread-local
- [ ] Any other lazy_static or OnceCell

### Type Thread Safety
For each public type, verify:
- [ ] Send + Sync if appropriate
- [ ] Documented thread safety guarantees
- [ ] No interior mutability surprises

### Specific Types to Verify
- [ ] Color: should be Send + Sync (no interior mutability)
- [ ] Style: should be Send + Sync
- [ ] Text: verify Send + Sync
- [ ] Segment: should be Send + Sync
- [ ] Console: may have interior mutability for recording
- [ ] Table, Panel, etc.: should be Send + Sync

### Caching Strategy
If using global caches:
```rust
// Option 1: Mutex (simple, some contention)
lazy_static! {
    static ref CACHE: Mutex<LruCache<String, Color>> = Mutex::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

// Option 2: Thread-local (no contention, per-thread memory)
thread_local! {
    static CACHE: RefCell<LruCache<String, Color>> = RefCell::new(LruCache::new(256));
}

// Option 3: dashmap (concurrent HashMap, best for high contention)
static CACHE: Lazy<DashMap<String, Color>> = Lazy::new(DashMap::new);
```

### Testing
```rust
#[test]
fn test_color_is_send_sync() {
    fn assert_send_sync<T: Send + Sync>() {}
    assert_send_sync::<Color>();
}

#[test]
fn test_concurrent_color_parsing() {
    let handles: Vec<_> = (0..10)
        .map(|_| {
            std::thread::spawn(|| {
                for _ in 0..1000 {
                    Color::parse("red").unwrap();
                    Color::parse("#ff0000").unwrap();
                }
            })
        })
        .collect();
    
    for h in handles {
        h.join().unwrap();
    }
}
```

## Deliverables
1. Thread safety audit document
2. Tests verifying Send + Sync
3. Documentation of any thread-safety caveats","acceptance_criteria":"All types audited for thread safety. Documentation updated. Concurrent tests pass.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:49.199193872-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:47:49.199193872-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-0y1","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6os","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.438088602-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-0y1","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-8ut","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:48:04.480950632-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-dsh","title":"Console missing configurable output stream (file field)","description":"## Issue

Per RICH_SPEC.md Section 7.3, Console should have a `file: Box<dyn Write>` field for configurable output stream.

Current implementation always writes to stdout, lacking the ability to redirect output to files, buffers, or other streams.

## Impact
Low - most use cases write to stdout. However, this limits testability and advanced use cases like capturing output.

## Fix
1. Add `file: Box<dyn Write>` field to Console struct
2. Default to stdout
3. Add builder method to configure output stream
4. Update all write methods to use the configurable stream","status":"open","priority":4,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T23:45:51.27672134-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:46:04.858406541-05:00","labels":["console","implementation"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-e23","title":"Test Feature Flag Combinations","description":"Create comprehensive tests for all feature flag combinations to ensure no compilation or runtime issues.

FEATURE FLAGS (from Cargo.toml):
- default = []
- syntax = ["syntect"]
- markdown = ["pulldown-cmark"]
- json = ["serde_json"]
- full = ["syntax", "markdown", "json"]

COMBINATIONS TO TEST (2^3 = 8):
1. No features (default)
2. syntax only
3. markdown only
4. json only
5. syntax + markdown
6. syntax + json
7. markdown + json
8. full (all three)

TEST REQUIREMENTS:

1. COMPILATION TESTS:
   - All 8 combinations must compile without warnings
   - No unused import warnings in any combination
   - No missing dependency errors

2. CONDITIONAL EXPORT TESTS:
   - Verify prelude exports correct items per feature
   - Verify renderables/mod.rs exports correct modules
   - Test that feature-gated types are NOT available when feature disabled

3. RUNTIME BEHAVIOR TESTS:
   - Basic rendering works in all combinations
   - Feature-specific functionality works when enabled
   - No panics when feature modules are absent

4. CI MATRIX:
   Create GitHub Actions matrix testing all combinations:
   

5. DOCUMENTATION:
   - Document which features enable which functionality
   - Document any feature interactions or conflicts

LOGGING:
- Log which features are enabled at test startup
- Log any feature-conditional code paths taken

ACCEPTANCE CRITERIA:
- [ ] All 8 feature combinations compile
- [ ] All 8 combinations pass basic tests
- [ ] CI matrix configured and passing
- [ ] No conditional compilation warnings","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:33.94158072-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:46:21.210103032-05:00","labels":["ci","infrastructure","testing"]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-ehe","title":"Create E2E Test: Table Rendering Scenarios","description":"## Objective
Comprehensive E2E tests for Table rendering with detailed logging.

## Background
Tables are the most complex renderable with many interacting features: column sizing, row rendering, borders, alignment. E2E tests ensure all features work together.

## Test Scenarios

### Scenario 1: Simple Table
```rust
#[test]
fn test_e2e_simple_table() {
    let _guard = init_test_logging();
    let span = info_span!("e2e_simple_table");
    let _enter = span.enter();
    
    let table = Table::new()
        .add_column(Column::new("Name"))
        .add_column(Column::new("Age"))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Alice"), Cell::new("30")]))
        .add_row(Row::new(vec![Cell::new("Bob"), Cell::new("25")]));
    
    debug!(columns = 2, rows = 2, "Table structure");
    
    let console = Console::new().width(40);
    let output = console.render_to_string(&table);
    
    debug!(output = %output, "Rendered table");
    
    // Verify structure
    assert!(output.contains("Name"), "Missing header");
    assert!(output.contains("Alice"), "Missing data");
    assert!(output.lines().count() >= 4, "Insufficient lines");
    
    info!(lines = output.lines().count(), "Table rendered successfully");
}
```

### Scenario 2: Table with All Features
- Title and caption
- Header and footer
- Multiple box styles
- Styled cells
- Vertical alignment in cells

### Scenario 3: Width Constraints
- Table wider than terminal (must collapse)
- Table narrower than terminal (must expand)
- Columns at minimum width
- Ratio-based column distribution

### Scenario 4: Wide Characters in Cells
- CJK characters
- Emoji
- Mixed ASCII/CJK

### Scenario 5: Multi-line Cells
- Cells with newlines
- Cells that wrap
- Vertical alignment with multi-line

### Scenario 6: Edge Cases
- Empty table
- Single cell
- Very narrow width (force minimum)
- Very wide content

## Logging Requirements
Each test must log:
1. **Setup**: Table structure (columns, rows, options)
2. **Width calculation**: Input widths, available width, result widths
3. **Row rendering**: Cell content, alignment applied
4. **Border drawing**: Box style, characters used
5. **Final output**: Complete rendered string
6. **Timing**: Time for width calc, time for render

## Golden Files
Create golden files for each scenario to detect visual regressions.

## Files to Create
- tests/e2e_table.rs
- tests/golden/table_*.txt","acceptance_criteria":"All table scenarios pass. Width algorithms verified. Logs show calculation details.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:44:29.700982578-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T12:37:41.691805306-05:00","close_reason":"Completed 37 Table E2E tests covering all scenarios: simple tables, full features, width constraints, wide characters, alignment, edge cases, and snapshot tests","labels":["e2e","testing"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-6d6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:45:12.880043097-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ehe","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-4ot","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T11:11:54.007910488-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-f6v","title":"Implement Columns renderable","description":"Implement a Columns renderable for arranging items in multiple columns (newspaper-style layout).

Features:
- Configurable number of columns

 {"id":"rich_rust-g92","title":"Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1","description":"# Validate Color System against RICH_SPEC Section 1

## Context
The Color System is foundational to rich_rust. It handles color representation, parsing, palette management, and ANSI code generation.

## Spec Requirements to Validate (from RICH_SPEC.md §1)

### 1.1 Data Structures
- [ ] ColorTriplet: `hex()`, `rgb()`, `normalized()` methods
- [ ] ColorSystem enum: STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] ColorType enum: DEFAULT (0), STANDARD (1), EIGHT_BIT (2), TRUECOLOR (3), WINDOWS (4)
- [ ] Color struct: name, color_type, number, triplet fields

### 1.2 Color Parsing (CRITICAL)
- [ ] Named colors: "red", "bright_blue" → STANDARD/EIGHT_BIT
- [ ] Hex format: "#FF0000" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Color number: "color(196)" → STANDARD if 0-15, EIGHT_BIT if 16-255
- [ ] RGB format: "rgb(255,0,0)" → TRUECOLOR
- [ ] Default: "default" → ColorType::DEFAULT
- [ ] Case-insensitivity
- [ ] Whitespace handling in rgb()
- [ ] **LRU cache (max 1024 entries)** - verify caching exists

### 1.3 Color Palettes
- [ ] STANDARD_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT_PALETTE: 256 colors (16 standard + 216 cube + 24 grayscale)
- [ ] WINDOWS_PALETTE: 16 colors with exact RGB values
- [ ] 6x6x6 cube formula: `16 + 36 * red_index + 6 * green_index + blue_index`
- [ ] Grayscale ramp: indices 232-255 with values (8,8,8) to (238,238,238)

### 1.4 Color Conversion Algorithms
- [ ] RGB to 8-bit: grayscale detection via HLS saturation < 0.15
- [ ] RGB to Standard: weighted CIE76 color distance formula
- [ ] Verify exact formulas match spec

### 1.5 ANSI Code Generation
- [ ] DEFAULT: ["39"] foreground, ["49"] background
- [ ] STANDARD 0-7: ["30"+n] fg, ["40"+n] bg
- [ ] STANDARD 8-15: ["82"+n] fg, ["92"+n] bg
- [ ] EIGHT_BIT: ["38", "5", "N"] fg, ["48", "5", "N"] bg
- [ ] TRUECOLOR: ["38", "2", "R", "G", "B"] fg, ["48", "2", "R", "G", "B"] bg

## Files to Check
- `src/color.rs` (955 lines)

## Validation Approach
1. Read src/color.rs thoroughly
2. Compare each implementation detail against spec
3. Create tests for any uncovered edge cases
4. Document discrepancies
5. Fix implementation or update spec as needed

## Acceptance Criteria
- All spec requirements validated with tests
- LRU caching verified or implemented
- Color conversion algorithms match exactly
- ANSI code generation matches spec table","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:13:47.105219826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:34:03.392808606-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:34:03.392811762-05:00","close_reason":"Color System validation complete - all RICH_SPEC Section 1 requirements verified:
- ColorTriplet, ColorSystem, ColorType, Color data structures match spec
- Color parsing (named, hex, rgb, color(N), default) implemented with LRU cache (1024 entries)
- STANDARD/WINDOWS/EIGHT_BIT palettes have correct RGB values
- 6x6x6 color cube formula: 16 + 36*r + 6*g + b verified
- Grayscale ramp (232-255) with values (8,8,8) to (238,238,238) verified
- rgb_to_eight_bit uses HLS saturation < 0.15 for grayscale detection
- ANSI code generation matches spec for all color types","labels":["phase-1","validation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-g92","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:19:35.645784979-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-gk4","title":"collapse_widths() missing rounding error correction loop","description":"## Minor Discrepancy Found

**Location**: src/renderables/table.rs:574-610

**Spec Requirement (RICH_SPEC Section 9.3, lines 1680-1694)**:
After proportional shrinking, there should be an explicit rounding error correction loop that removes any remaining excess from the largest shrinkable column.

**Actual Behavior**:
The implementation uses inline `remaining_excess` tracking but does not have the post-loop correction from the spec.

**Impact**:
Minor - may result in off-by-one differences in edge cases where integer division rounding doesn't perfectly distribute the excess.

**Validation Test**:
See tests/e2e_width_algorithms.rs

**Recommended Fix**:
Add the rounding correction loop from spec lines 1680-1694 after the main proportional shrink loop.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:32.977555554-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:32:06.073256642-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:32:06.073256642-05:00","close_reason":"Fixed: Added rounding error correction loop per RICH_SPEC Section 9.3 (lines 1680-1694). After proportional shrinking, implementation now checks if new_total > available and removes excess from columns in reverse order. Tests pass (test_collapse_widths_proportional_shrink). Implementation at table.rs:680-695.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-gk4","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q1k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T13:14:39.089609282-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}

 {"id":"rich_rust-k5a","title":"Validate Padding Component","description":"Validate Padding component against RICH_SPEC.md and Python Rich source.

- [ ] PaddingDimensions from (vertical, horizontal) tuple
- [ ] PaddingDimensions from (top, right, bottom, left) tuple
- [ ] Top padding adds blank lines before content
- [ ] Bottom padding adds blank lines after content
- [ ] Left padding prepends spaces to each line
- [ ] Right padding appends spaces to each line (if expand=true)
- [ ] Padding respects available width constraints
- [ ] Nested padding accumulates correctly
- [ ] Zero padding edge case (no-op)
- [ ] Asymmetric padding (e.g., 0,2,0,2 for horizontal only)

KEY BEHAVIORS:
- Left/right padding affects cell width calculations
- Top/bottom padding affects line count
- Padding content is unstyled (plain spaces)

 {"id":"rich_rust-kko","title":"Validate Style System against RICH_SPEC Section 2","description":"## Objective
Verify the Style implementation in src/style.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 2 exactly.

## Background
The Style system is fundamental to rich_rust - it encapsulates visual attributes (colors, bold, italic, etc.) that are applied to text. The RICH_SPEC defines 13 attributes using bitflags and specific combine/override semantics.

## Validation Checklist

### 2.1 Attributes Bitflags
- [ ] Verify all 13 attributes defined: BOLD, DIM, ITALIC, UNDERLINE, BLINK, BLINK2, REVERSE, CONCEAL, STRIKE, UNDERLINE2, FRAME, ENCIRCLE, OVERLINE
- [ ] Verify bit positions match spec (BOLD=1, DIM=2, ITALIC=4, etc.)
- [ ] Verify set() and unset() methods work correctly
- [ ] Verify is_set() method returns correct boolean

### 2.2 Style Struct
- [ ] Verify fields: color (Option<Color>), bgcolor (Option<Color>), attributes (Attributes), link (Option<String>), meta (Option<HashMap>)
- [ ] Verify Style::null() creates empty style (all None, empty attributes)
- [ ] Verify Style::new() creates null style
- [ ] Verify builder methods: bold(), italic(), underline(), strike(), dim(), reverse(), blink(), conceal(), overline()
- [ ] Verify color() and on() methods for foreground/background colors
- [ ] Verify link() method for hyperlinks (OSC 8 support)

### 2.3 Style Combination
- [ ] Verify combine(&other) merges styles with other taking precedence
- [ ] Test: null.combine(bold) = bold
- [ ] Test: bold.combine(italic) = bold+italic
- [ ] Test: red.combine(blue) = blue (color override)
- [ ] Verify attribute union: self.attributes | other.attributes

### 2.4 Style Parsing
- [ ] Verify Style::parse() handles named colors
- [ ] Verify attribute keywords: bold, dim, italic, underline, blink, reverse, conceal, strike, overline
- [ ] Verify 'on' keyword for background
- [ ] Verify 'not' prefix for unsetting
- [ ] Verify 'link=URL' syntax for hyperlinks

### 2.5 ANSI Code Generation
- [ ] Verify render_to_ansi() produces correct SGR sequences
- [ ] Verify color system downgrade: TrueColor->EightBit->Standard
- [ ] Verify reset code (0) when style is null

## Files to Examine
- src/style.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 2","acceptance_criteria":"All checklist items verified and documented. Any discrepancies logged as separate bug/task beads.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:16:40.607570332-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:37.493238226-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:39:37.493238226-05:00","close_reason":"Validation complete.  {"id":"rich_rust-ms5","title":"Verify Table Width Collapse Algorithm","description":"## Objective
Verify collapse_widths() in Table matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 9 exactly.

## Background
When table content exceeds available width, collapse_widths() must reduce column widths proportionally while respecting minimum widths. This is a complex algorithm with many edge cases.

## Technical Details

### Spec Requirement (Section 9.4)
collapse_widths() should:
1. Calculate excess = current_total - available_width
2. Distribute reduction proportionally to (current - minimum)
3. Never reduce below minimum width
4. Handle columns that hit minimum (redistribute to others)

### Algorithm Verification
```
Given: 3 columns with widths [40, 30, 30] = 100
       minimums [10, 10, 10]
       available width = 70
       excess = 30

Step 1: Calculate reducible space per column
  col0: 40-10 = 30 reducible
  col1: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  col2: 30-10 = 20 reducible
  total reducible = 70

Step 2: Distribute reduction proportionally
  col0 reduces by: 30 * (30/70) = ~12.9
  col1 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6
  col2 reduces by: 30 * (20/70) = ~8.6

Result: [27, 21, 22] (with rounding)
```

### Test Cases
1. All columns have same reducible space
2. One column at minimum (can't reduce further)
3. All columns at minimum
4. Single column table
5. Very narrow width (forces all to minimum)

## Files to Examine
- src/renderables/table.rs (collapse_widths function)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 9.4","acceptance_criteria":"collapse_widths matches spec. All test cases pass.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:21:58.81508283-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T23:37:04.558811593-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-17T23:37:04.558811593-05:00","close_reason":"Validated: collapse_widths() now matches RICH_SPEC Section 9.3 exactly. Includes:
- Proportional reduction based on (current - minimum) ratios
- Post-loop rounding correction (spec lines 1680-1694)
Tests pass. Implementation at table.rs:646-698.","labels":["critical","implementation"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-a96","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:23:03.781926826-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"},{"issue_id":"rich_rust-ms5","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-q5f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:46:58.046952534-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
 {"id":"rich_rust-mst","title":"Validate Box Characters against RICH_SPEC Section 8","description":"## Objective
Verify the Box implementation in src/box.rs matches RICH_SPEC.md Section 8 exactly.

## Background
Box characters are used to draw borders around tables and panels. The spec defines 8 character positions per row and 8 row types, plus built-in styles (ASCII, ROUNDED, DOUBLE, etc.).

## Validation Checklist

- [ ] Total: 64 character positions

### 8.2 Built-in Box Styles
- [ ] Verify ASCII box: +, -, |, etc.
- [ ] Verify ASCII2 box: variations
- [ ] Verify ASCII_DOUBLE_HEAD: double top line
- [ ] Verify ROUNDED box: rounded corners (╭╮╰╯)
- [ ] Verify SQUARE box: sharp corners (┌┐└┘)
- [ ] Verify DOUBLE box: double lines (═║╔╗╚╝)
- [ ] Verify HEAVY box: heavy lines (━┃┏┓┗┛)
- [ ] Verify HEAVY_HEAD: heavy top only
- [ ] Verify MINIMAL box: minimal borders
- [ ] Verify SIMPLE box: simple lines
- [ ] Verify SIMPLE_HEAVY: simple with heavy lines

### 8.3 Box Methods
- [ ] Verify get_top() returns top row chars
- [ ] Verify get_row() returns content row chars
- [ ] Verify get_bottom() returns bottom row chars
- [ ] Verify substitute(safe_box) for ASCII fallback

### 8.4 Safe Box Mode
- [ ] Verify safe_box=true falls back to ASCII
- [ ] Verify non-ASCII terminals get ASCII automatically
- [ ] Verify detection of Unicode support

## Files to Examine
- src/box.rs (primary)
- RICH_SPEC.md Section 8","acceptance_criteria":"All 11 box styles verified.  {"id":"rich_rust-nqh","title":"Create Migration Guide from Python Rich","description":"## Objective
Create a migration guide helping Python Rich users adopt rich_rust.

## Guide Contents

### Feature Mapping Table
| Python Rich | rich_rust | Notes |
|-------------|-----------|-------|
| console.print() | Console::print() | Same semantics |
| Text() | Text::new() | Similar API |
| Style() | Style::new() | Builder pattern |
| Table() | Table::new() | Similar API |
| Panel() | Panel::new() | Similar API |
| Markdown() | Markdown::parse() | Feature flag |
| Syntax() | Syntax::new() | Feature flag |
| Live() | Not implemented | Excluded |
| Status() | Not implemented | Excluded |

### API Differences
- Python: console.print(text, style='bold')
- Rust: console.print('[bold]text[/]');

### Markup Syntax
- Same: [bold], [red], [on blue], [/]
- Same: Escaping with backslash

### Console Options
- force_terminal -> force_terminal
- record -> record
- width -> width()
- color_system -> color_system()

### Common Patterns

Python:
```python
console = Console()
table = Table(title='My Table')
table.add_column('Name')
table.add_row('Alice')
console.print(table)
```

Rust:
```rust
let console = Console::new();
let table = Table::new()
    .title('My Table')
    .add_column(Column::new('Name'))
    .add_row(Row::new(vec\![Cell::new('Alice')]));
console.print(table);
```

### Feature Flags
- syntax: Syntax highlighting (requires syntect)
- markdown: Markdown rendering (requires pulldown-cmark)
- json: JSON rendering (requires serde_json)

## Files to Create
- docs/MIGRATION.md","acceptance_criteria":"Migration guide covers all shared features. Code examples work.","status":"open","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:37.84434352-05:00","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"rich_rust-nqh","depends_on_id":"rich_rust-2oy","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-17T10:25:48.024395587-05:00","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone"}]}
